name: Apple Brown Betty with Rum Whipped Cream
recipeIngredient: 3 Granny Smith apples; peeled, cored, and cut into 1-inch pieces (about 1-1/2 pounds)
8 tbsp (1 stick) unsalted butter, chilled and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. Butter a 9-inch-square baking dish.
3. Sift the flour, salt, cinnamon, nutmeg, and sugar into a bowl.
4. Cut the butter into small pieces and combine it with the flour mixture using a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
5. Cut the bread into 1-inch square pieces.
6. Soak the raisins in boiling water for about 5 minutes, then drain them.
7. Combine the bread, raisins, and sliced apples in a bowl.
8. Sprinkle the bottom of the prepared baking dish with 3/4 cup of the flour mixture, add the apple mixture, and top with the remaining flour mixture.
9. Bake, uncovered, in the preheated oven until the top of the brown betty is crisp and golden and the apples are tender, about 35 to 45 minutes.
10. To make the rum whipped cream: In a chilled medium bowl, beat the cream and sugar until stiff. Beat in the rum.
11. Serve the betty warm, with the rum whipped cream. Serves 6.
